references.text_excerpt: They departed into the wilderness to go up to the land of Nephi.
Ammon was carried into the land of Ishmael.
Aaron went to the city of Jerusalem.
They were cast into prison in Midoni.
Lamanite possessions were divided from Zarahemla by a wilderness, from the east sea to the head of Sidon by Manti, with Nephi on the south.
The seashore on the west of Nephi, by the wilderness, was a place of the Lamanites.
ambiguity_flags: west-side coastal relation may need later regional refinement

references.text_excerpt: Nephites had inhabited Bountiful from the east sea to the west sea; Bountiful bordered Desolation at the narrow pass leading northward.
Desolation was so named because of the destruction of the Jaredites, and it bordered the land northward.
Bountiful and Desolation were nearly surrounded by water, with a small neck between the land northward and the land southward.
ambiguity_flags: small neck may overlap conceptually with narrow pass

references.text_excerpt: The narrow neck was only a day and a half's journey for a Nephite, from east to west sea.
ambiguity_flags: distance estimate is narrative and interpretive

references.text_excerpt: Nephites occupied the north in Bountiful to keep Lamanites from reaching the land northward.
ambiguity_flags: north in Bountiful may describe sector rather than separate node

references.text_excerpt: Thus the Lamanites were confined south of Bountiful in the land southward.
They gathered again in Zarahemla.
The Zoramites and Lamanites prepared war against Jershon and surrounding Nephite lands.
The Lamanites were brought around by the wilderness toward the river Sidon crossing zone.
Those who fled from the battle departed into the wilderness and toward Manti.
Moroni had fortified the line from Nephihah to Lehi and beyond.
Moroni placed fortifications by the east wilderness and the line of cities including Lehi and Morianton.
A city was founded between the city of Moroni and the city of Aaron, joining the borders of Aaron and Moroni, and it was called Nephihah.
The line continued through Gid and on to Mulek.
Teancum marched from Mulek toward Bountiful to stop Amalickiah.
Teancum camped by the seashore in the borders of Bountiful.
The Nephites retained the advantage in Bountiful and the narrow passage northward.
A decoy force moved from Mulek toward the seashore.
Moroni and Teancum recovered Mulek; the Lamanites were pursued near the city Bountiful, which Lehi had been left to protect.
Moroni planned the recovery operation at Gid.
The city of Nephihah had fallen to the Lamanites.
Helaman entered the southern campaign theater involving Judea and Antiparah.
Helaman's young men drew the enemy into the wilderness route away from Antiparah.
The city of Cumeni was brought into Nephite control.
The campaign advanced toward Manti and secured it.
Moroni moved down from Gideon toward Zarahemla to assist Pahoran.
After Zarahemla was regained, the campaign redirected toward eastern cities including Nephihah.
The city of Zarahemla was burned with fire.
The city of Moroni sank into the depths of the sea.
The earth was carried up upon the city of Moronihah.
The whole face of the land was changed.
A multitude gathered round about the temple in the land Bountiful.
Jesus commanded the multitude to go to their homes and prepare for the morrow.
The multitude spread abroad upon all the face of the land and did greatly publish what they had seen.
The Father gathered and established his people in this land.
Many departed out of the land of Zarahemla and went forth unto the land northward to inherit the land.
They did go forth from the land southward to the land northward, from the sea south to the sea north, from the sea west to the sea east.
references.review_notes: Frame-defining verse for sea north/sea south.

references.text_excerpt: The people who were in the land northward did dwell in tents, and in houses of cement.
references.review_notes: Supports active habitation in land northward.

references.text_excerpt: Timber was exceedingly scarce in the land northward, and much was sent by the way of shipping.
references.review_notes: Useful for later maritime logistics layer.

references.text_excerpt: They fortified from the west sea even unto the east, on the line defending their north country.
references.review_notes: Keeps eastern endpoint conservative for now.

references.text_excerpt: The dissenters and Lamanites had obtained all the possession of the Nephites which was in the land southward.
references.review_notes: Broadens evidence base for land southward.

references.text_excerpt: Many Lamanites and also Nephi and Lehi went into the land northward.
references.review_notes: Confirms continued movement into land northward.

references.text_excerpt: Nephi returned to the land of Zarahemla from the land northward.
The Amlicites came upon the hill Amnihu, which was east of the river Sidon, which ran by the land of Zarahemla.
Alma caused his people to pitch their tents in the valley of Gideon.
In the land of Minon, above the land of Zarahemla, in the course of the land of Nephi, a numerous host of the Lamanites was seen.
The people departed out of the valley of Gideon towards their city, which was the city of Zarahemla.
As they were crossing the river Sidon ... Alma cleared the west bank of Sidon and crossed to contend with the enemy on the west side of the river Sidon.
The fleeing armies were scattered west and north until they reached the wilderness called Hermounts, a wilderness infested by wild and ravenous beasts.
references.review_notes: Direct naming of the wilderness of Hermounts.

references.text_excerpt: Alma departed from the city of Zarahemla and went over upon the east of the river Sidon, into the valley of Gideon.
Zoram and his sons crossed over the river Sidon and marched beyond the borders of Manti into the south wilderness, which was on the east side of the river Sidon.
Moroni drove the Lamanites from the east wilderness and fortified the line between the land of Zarahemla and the land of Nephi.
Pahoran fled to the land of Gideon; Zarahemla had been taken; Moroni proposed taking possession of the city of Zarahemla.
Moroni marched toward the land of Gideon, then went down with his armies into Zarahemla, and later marched toward the land of Nephihah.
Moroni had fortified the line from the city of Nephihah to the city of Lehi, and from the city of Lehi unto the city of Morianton.
The line of cities continued from Lehi and Morianton, by Omner, Gid, and Mulek, all of which were on the east borders by the seashore.
There was a border dispute between Lehi and Morianton; Morianton fled toward the land northward, but was intercepted by Teancum before reaching the land Bountiful.
Teancum returned to the city Bountiful to await Moroni, then the Mulek operation drew forces toward the seashore.
Moroni devised a recovery operation at the city and land of Gid, while the east city chain remained strategically linked.
Moroni regained the city and land of Nephihah.
Helaman entered the southern campaign theater involving Judea, Antiparah, Cumeni, and Manti.
There was no way to lead provisions to Helaman save it were by way of the land of Zarahemla and from the land round about unto the land of Manti.
Helaman's sons drew the Lamanites away from Antiparah into the wilderness and toward the sea, while the path back led toward Cumeni and Manti.
The city and land of Cumeni were brought into Nephite control in the southern campaign.
Helaman advanced from Cumeni toward Manti and eventually obtained possession of the city and land of Manti.
Mosiah sent men from Zarahemla to inquire concerning the people in the land and city of Lehi-Nephi.
Ammon's party wandered in the wilderness and came to a hill north of the land of Shilom.
Ammon went down into the land of Nephi.
The king of the people in the land of Nephi and in the land of Shilom held Ammon's party.
Limhi referred to the city of Lehi-Nephi, the city of Shilom, and the land round about yielded by treaty.
Zeniff received the land of Lehi-Nephi and the land of Shilom, and repaired the city walls of Lehi-Nephi and Shilom.
The city of Lehi-Nephi functioned as a refuge during Lamanite attack south of Shilom.
Zeniff set spies round about Shemlon; Lamanites came upon the north of Shilom.
Alma gathered believers at the place of Mormon, by the waters of Mormon.
Alma's people fled eight days into the wilderness and came to a very beautiful and pleasant land.
Alma's people prospered in the land of Helam and built the city of Helam.
The Lamanites found Amulon, then discovered the land and city of Helam while searching for the land of Nephi.
Amulon ruled over Helam and over the lands of Shemlon, Shilom, and Amulon.
Alma's people departed from Helam, camped in the valley of Alma, then reached Zarahemla after twelve days in the wilderness.
Moroni gives an account of the ancient inhabitants destroyed upon the face of this north country.
Omer departed, passed by the hill of Shim and the place where the Nephites were destroyed, then eastward to Ablom by the seashore.
Battle continued in the wilderness of Akish; Gilead then came forth to the land of Moron and placed himself upon the throne.
Coriantumr came up unto the land of Moron; Lib fled to the borders upon the seashore; pursuit continued to the wilderness of Akish and to the plains of Agosh.
Shiz pursued Coriantumr eastward to the borders by the seashore; they fled to the land of Corihor; camps were pitched in the valley of Corihor and the valley of Shurr near the hill Comnor.
They came to the waters of Ripliancum, which, by interpretation, is large, or to exceed all.
They pitched tents at Ramah, and it was that same hill where my father Mormon hid up the records unto the Lord, which was called Cumorah by the Nephites.
They gathered together all the people upon all the face of the land, who had not been slain, save it was Ether.
Mosiah was warned of the Lord and led his people out of the land of Nephi, down into the land which is called the land of Zarahemla.
Mosiah discovered the people of Zarahemla and dwelt with them in the land of Zarahemla.
A company desired to possess the land of their inheritance and went up again to the wilderness and to the land of Nephi.
King Benjamin reigned in the land of Zarahemla and gathered his people while contending with enemies.
King Benjamin thought it expedient that his sons should know concerning the land of Zarahemla and the people thereof.
The people gathered at the temple in the city of Zarahemla, and King Benjamin caused a tower to be erected.
After speaking from the tower, Benjamin dismissed the multitude and they returned to their own houses.
Mosiah began to reign in his father's stead in the land of Zarahemla.
Lehi dwelt at Jerusalem in all his days.
Lehi departed into the wilderness, came down by the borders near the shore of the Red Sea, and traveled in the wilderness in the borders nearer the Red Sea.
Lehi called the river Laman, which emptied into the Red Sea; the valley was called Lemuel.
They took their journey again in the wilderness and called the place Shazer; then traveled four days nearly a south-southeast direction.
Ishmael died and was buried in the place which was called Nahom.
They came to the land which they called Bountiful, by the seashore, and pitched their tents.
Laman entered the house of Laban in Jerusalem to ask for the plates.
The brothers returned to the land of Jerusalem, gathered gold and silver from the house of Lehi, and offered Laban's treasury.
Nephi urged his brothers to go up again unto Jerusalem, and he crept into the city by night.
Lehi sent his sons back to Jerusalem to bring down Ishmael and his household into the wilderness.
After traveling, they pitched tents for a time and Nephi's bow broke.
Nephi went into the mountain to pray at Bountiful and was told where to find ore to make tools.
The Lord showed Nephi how to build the ship; they worked timbers of curious workmanship.
They launched into the sea, were driven upon the great waters, and arrived in the promised land.
Nephi took his people and departed into the wilderness, journeyed many days, and called the new place Nephi, leaving the land of first inheritance.
Nephi built buildings, the temple, and the city was called Nephi.
Nephi consecrated Jacob and Joseph in the land of Nephi.
Prisoners were marched into the land, or city, Bountiful, and the city of Bountiful was encircled with fortifications.
references.review_notes: Important city/land same-name distinction for Bountiful.

references.text_excerpt: Prisoners were taken from the city Gid to the city Bountiful, which was guarded with an exceedingly strong force.
Nephi and Lehi began at the city Bountiful, then went to the city of Gid and from Gid to the city of Mulek.
references.review_notes: Useful direct city sequence for the eastern corridor.

references.text_excerpt: Jacob taught in the temple after Nephi's death.
That great city Zarahemla was caused to be built again.
references.review_notes: Direct post-Christ continuity anchor for Zarahemla.

references.text_excerpt: Lamoni refused to return to Ishmael and instead went to Middoni to release Ammon's brethren.
references.review_notes: Strong travel contrast between Ishmael and Middoni.

references.text_excerpt: Ammon and Lamoni proceeded toward Middoni, where the imprisoned brethren were brought forth after many afflictions.
references.review_notes: Confirms Middoni as the imprisonment and release destination.

references.text_excerpt: Jerusalem was a Lamanite city built in a land of the same name, away joining the borders of Mormon.
references.review_notes: Best direct location statement for the Lamanite Jerusalem.

references.text_excerpt: Nephi departed out of the land of Zarahemla and entrusted the records to his son.
Nephi did not return to the land of Zarahemla and could not be found in all the land.
The appointed gathering land was Zarahemla and the land between Zarahemla and Bountiful up to the line between Bountiful and Desolation.
The people gathered in the land southward because of the great curse upon the land northward.
Zemnarihah ordered his people to withdraw and march into the furthermost parts of the land northward.
They returned to their own lands and possessions on both the land northward and the land southward.
references.review_notes: Direct paired reference to northward and southward lands.

references.text_excerpt: A complaint came up unto the land of Zarahemla, to the governor of the land, against the judges.
There was terrible destruction in the land southward, but even greater destruction in the land northward.
references.review_notes: Direct destruction-era contrast between northward and southward lands.

references.text_excerpt: The Amlicites were slain upon the hill east of Sidon.
Those slain upon the bank of the river Sidon were cast into the waters of Sidon.
references.review_notes: Direct river bank and waters reference.

references.text_excerpt: Another Lamanite army came in upon the people after the battle fought in the land of Zarahemla.
references.review_notes: Explicit Zarahemla land reference in campaign context.

references.text_excerpt: There were no contentions nor wars in the land of Zarahemla in the sixth year of the judges.
Many were baptized in the waters of Sidon and joined the church of God.
references.review_notes: Direct waters-of-Sidon reference normalized to river Sidon.

references.text_excerpt: Alma journeyed into Melek on the west of the river Sidon, by the borders of the wilderness.
references.review_notes: Direct Melek travel verse with explicit west-of-Sidon placement.

references.text_excerpt: They went up to the land of Nephi to preach the word of God unto the Lamanites.
references.review_notes: Explicit land of Nephi mission reference.

references.text_excerpt: Lamoni was to be conducted to the land of Nephi for a great feast appointed there.
references.review_notes: Explicit land of Nephi destination reference.

references.text_excerpt: Lamoni desired that Ammon should go with him to the land of Nephi.
references.review_notes: Explicit land of Nephi destination reference.

references.text_excerpt: Ammon was told not to go up to the land of Nephi but to go to the land of Middoni.
references.review_notes: Direct travel contrast between Nephi and Middoni.

references.text_excerpt: Aaron departed from the land of Middoni and was led to the land of Nephi, over all the land save the land of Ishmael.
The covenant invoked brethren in the land northward as a warning example.
Alma began preaching first in the land of Zarahemla and then throughout the land.
references.review_notes: Direct but mainly contextual Zarahemla reference.

references.text_excerpt: Alma returned from Gideon after earlier labor in the land of Zarahemla and came back to his house at Zarahemla.
Zeezrom lay sick at Sidom, and hearing that Alma and Amulek were there, he sent for them.
references.review_notes: Direct Sidom city reference in the Zeezrom healing sequence.

references.text_excerpt: When Zeezrom heard that Alma and Amulek were in the land of Sidom, he took courage and sent for them.
The knowledge of Zeezrom's healing went forth throughout all the land of Sidom.
references.review_notes: Direct land of Sidom spread-of-knowledge verse.

references.text_excerpt: Many came from the region round about to Sidom and were baptized after the church was established there.
references.review_notes: Direct Sidom mission-context reference after the Ammonihah ordeal.

references.text_excerpt: After laboring in Sidom, Alma brought Amulek over to the land of Zarahemla.
references.review_notes: Moderately useful movement reference into Zarahemla.

references.text_excerpt: There had been peace in the land of Zarahemla before a cry of war was heard throughout the land.
As Alma journeyed from Gideon toward Manti, he met the sons of Mosiah traveling toward the land of Zarahemla.
references.review_notes: Useful directional journey reference involving Zarahemla.

references.text_excerpt: The sons of Mosiah departed out of the land of Zarahemla and traveled into the wilderness.
Lamanite armies went into the borders of the land of Zarahemla and attacked the people in Ammonihah.
Ammon recalls coming up out of the land of Zarahemla to begin the mission.
references.review_notes: Origin reference for Zarahemla in missionary context.

references.text_excerpt: The missionaries said to their brethren in the land of Zarahemla that they would go up to the land of Nephi.
The people of the Lord propose going down to the land of Zarahemla to the Nephites.
The people departed and came into the wilderness dividing the land of Nephi from the land of Zarahemla, near the borders of the land.
ambiguity_flags: dividing wilderness likely broader than current border wilderness proxy

references.text_excerpt: Ammon said he and his brethren would go forth into the land of Zarahemla while the people waited.
Alma conducted his brethren back to the land of Zarahemla, where they reported what had happened in the land of Nephi.
After the people of Ammon were established in Jershon, Nephite armies were set round about Jershon and the borders round about the land of Zarahemla.
A man came into the land of Zarahemla and preached against the prophecies concerning Christ.
Korihor was sent to the land of Zarahemla to be brought before Alma and the chief judge.
Antionum is described as east of Zarahemla, south of Jershon, and near the seashore.
references.review_notes: High-value relational verse linking Antionum, Zarahemla, and Jershon.

references.text_excerpt: Alma and his companions returned to the land of Zarahemla while repentant Zoramites received lands in Jershon.
Alma departed out of the land of Zarahemla as if to go into the land of Melek.
Alma taught throughout the land of Melek, and from there traveled north of Melek to the city called Ammonihah.
references.review_notes: Strong repeated Melek land reference with northward travel relation toward Ammonihah.

references.text_excerpt: The people of Ammon departed out of Jershon and came over into the land of Melek, giving place in Jershon for the armies.
Moroni's prisoners were taken back into the land of Zarahemla after Amalickiah fled.
The king's servants fled through the wilderness and came over into the land of Zarahemla.
The army moved through the wilderness toward the land of Zarahemla.
Amalickiah feared not to come down to the land of Zarahemla with a great army.
Ammoron had departed out of the land of Zarahemla and marched toward the west sea borders.
The people of Ammon had been brought down into the land of Zarahemla and protected by the Nephites.
Lamanites came up to the land of Nephi to destroy the king and the people of Anti-Nephi-Lehi.
Many Lamanites came to dwell in the land of Ishmael and the land of Nephi after returning to their own land.
The Lamanites returned again to the land of Nephi after failing to destroy the Anti-Nephi-Lehies.
The record recounts Ammon and his brethren's journeyings and sufferings in the land of Nephi and the safety of the brethren in Jershon.
references.review_notes: Summary continuity verse linking Nephi and Jershon.

references.text_excerpt: Alma rejoices in the success of his brethren who had been up to the land of Nephi.
references.review_notes: Contextual but direct Nephi mission reference.

references.text_excerpt: Amalickiah departed into the land of Nephi to strengthen the Lamanites against the Nephites.
references.review_notes: Direct Nephi destination in Amalickiah narrative.

references.text_excerpt: Amalickiah went up in the land of Nephi among the Lamanites and stirred them to anger.
references.review_notes: Direct Nephi setting for Amalickiah's Lamanite mobilization.

references.text_excerpt: Amalickiah marched to the land of Nephi, to the city of Nephi, the chief city.
The Lamanites returned to the land of Nephi to report their loss to Amalickiah.
Moroni took the city of Mulek, one of the strongest Lamanite holds in the land of Nephi.
references.review_notes: Useful land-level framing for Mulek within Nephi's possession zone.

references.text_excerpt: Ammoron identifies the land of Nephi as the Lamanites' land of possessions.
references.review_notes: Strong identity statement for Nephi as core Lamanite possession.

references.text_excerpt: The sons of those brought down out of the land of Nephi are introduced before the military campaign account.
references.review_notes: Contextual but direct Nephi origin reference.

references.text_excerpt: Helaman supposes the spared prisoners are now in the land of Nephi.
references.review_notes: Contextual but direct Nephi military reference.

references.text_excerpt: The Lamanites fled to the land of Nephi while the Nephites retained their lands.
Dissenters deserted away into the land of Nephi among the Lamanites.
Nephi and Lehi proceeded to go to the land of Nephi.
The search party was met in the land of Nephi and in the land of Shilom and taken prisoner.
Zeniff knew the land of Nephi, also called the land of their fathers' first inheritance, before returning to Zarahemla.
references.review_notes: Key early Nephi / first inheritance linkage verse.

references.text_excerpt: The captives were carried back to the land of Nephi and allowed to possess the land under tribute.
The people swore that they would return to the land of Nephi if their families had been slain.
The people were about to return to the land of Nephi when they met the men of Gideon.
ambiguity_flags: Gideon reference not normalized here because the text does not explicitly say land of Gideon

references.text_excerpt: After the ceremony the people returned to the land of Nephi rejoicing.
The Lamanite king led his people up to the land of Nephi to destroy the people of Limhi.
The intruders had come into the land of Nephi by night and carried off grain and precious things.
Limhi's people departed by night, went round about the land of Shilom in the wilderness, and bent their course toward the land of Zarahemla.
After many days in the wilderness, Limhi's people arrived in the land of Zarahemla.
references.review_notes: Direct Zarahemla arrival reference from Limhi escape narrative.

references.text_excerpt: Limhi's search party failed to find Zarahemla and returned to the land of Nephi after reaching the borders of a destroyed land.
ambiguity_flags: Destroyed land reference not normalized here

references.text_excerpt: Alma is told to remember the captivity of his fathers in the land of Helam and in the land of Nephi.
The sons of Mosiah desired to go up to the land of Nephi to preach to the Lamanites.
references.review_notes: Direct mission departure verse to Nephi.

references.text_excerpt: The sons of Mosiah pleaded many days that they might go up to the land of Nephi.
Aaron had gone up to the land of Nephi and therefore could not receive the kingdom.
The Lamanite armies came down out of the land of Nephi to battle against Benjamin's people.
The Lord said the Lamanites would cross the river Sidon in the south wilderness beyond the borders of Manti, on the east of the river.
A concealed force waited on the west of the river Sidon and toward the borders of Manti.
ambiguity_flags: West valley not normalized here because no current node exists

references.text_excerpt: The Lamanites began to cross the river Sidon and were encircled from the east by Lehi's army.
ambiguity_flags: Hill Riplah not normalized here because no current node exists

references.text_excerpt: The Lamanites fled toward the river Sidon because of the great destruction among them.
The Lamanites were driven into the waters of Sidon and crossed while Lehi held the bank of the river Sidon.
Moroni met the Lamanites in the valley on the other side of the river Sidon and began to slay them.
ambiguity_flags: Unnamed valley not normalized here because no stable node exists

references.text_excerpt: The Lamanites fled before the Nephites even to the waters of Sidon.
The Lamanites were gathered in one body in the valley upon the bank by the river Sidon.
ambiguity_flags: Unnamed valley/bank scene normalized only to Sidon crossing zone

references.text_excerpt: Zerahemnah saw Lehi on the east of the river Sidon and Moroni on the west of the river Sidon.
references.review_notes: High-value east/west Sidon battle geometry verse.

references.text_excerpt: Lehi, captain over that city, was the same Lehi who fought with the Lamanites in the valley on the east of the river Sidon.
ambiguity_flags: City Ammonihah / City Noah not normalized here because no current node exists

references.text_excerpt: Moroni fortified the line between Zarahemla and Nephi from the west sea, running by the head of the river Sidon, while the Nephites possessed land northward of Bountiful.
The Lamanites dared not cross the head of Sidon over to the city of Nephihah or march down against Zarahemla.
War began in the borders of Zarahemla by the waters of Sidon.
The people of Ammon had been brought down into the land of Zarahemla and protected by the Nephites.
Two thousand men and provisions were sent from the land of Zarahemla.
Prisoners were sent to the land of Zarahemla while the army marched back to the city of Judea.
Provisions and an army addition were received from the land of Zarahemla and the land round about.
ambiguity_flags: land round about not normalized here

references.text_excerpt: Helaman planned to send provisions to Judea and prisoners to the land of Zarahemla.
The prisoners might need to be guarded down to the land of Zarahemla.
Helaman asked Gid about the prisoners who had started to go down to the land of Zarahemla.
An embassy was sent to the governor while Helaman waited for provisions and strength from the land of Zarahemla.
Helaman resolved to send the prisoners down to the land of Zarahemla under guard.
Gid reported that they had started to go down to the land of Zarahemla with the prisoners.
Helaman waited to receive more strength and provisions from the land of Zarahemla.
After traveling much in the wilderness, the army took its course toward the land of Zarahemla.
The Lamanites retreated when they saw the Nephites marching toward the land of Zarahemla.
Moroni sent an epistle to the land of Zarahemla and then planned to recover remaining possessions and cities.
Moroni warns that he will come to the land of Zarahemla if relief is not sent.
A large company departed out of the land of Zarahemla into the land northward.
Coriantumr was sent to march down to the land of Zarahemla to battle against the Nephites.
Insufficient guards had been kept in the land of Zarahemla because the Nephites did not expect an attack on that great city.
Coriantumr did not tarry in Zarahemla but marched toward the city of Bountiful to obtain the north parts of the land.
Before the invaders reached the land of Bountiful they were met in battle and retreated back toward Zarahemla.
There was continual rejoicing in the land of Zarahemla and the surrounding regions.
ambiguity_flags: regions round about not normalized here

references.text_excerpt: The Lamanites obtained possession of the land of Zarahemla and all lands even unto the land near Bountiful.
Nephi and Lehi went among the people in the land southward and then into the land of Zarahemla among the Lamanites.
Eight thousand Lamanites in the land of Zarahemla and round about were baptized unto repentance.
Many Lamanites came down into the land of Zarahemla and declared the manner of their conversion.
references.review_notes: Direct Zarahemla arrival verse after conversion.

references.text_excerpt: Samuel the Lamanite came into the land of Zarahemla and preached many days before being cast out.
references.review_notes: Samuel the Lamanite Zarahemla arrival/preaching verse.

references.text_excerpt: Mormon was carried by his father into the land southward, even to the land of Zarahemla.
There was no more contention in all the land of Zarahemla during the remainder of king Benjamin's days.
Zeniff had come up out of the land of Zarahemla to inherit the land of his fathers.
references.review_notes: Direct Zarahemla origin in Zeniff narrative.

references.text_excerpt: Ammon, a descendant of Zarahemla, came up out of the land of Zarahemla to inquire concerning Zeniff's people.
references.review_notes: Direct Zarahemla origin for Ammon expedition.

references.text_excerpt: Limhi rejoiced that his brethren in the land of Zarahemla were still alive.
Limhi told his people all the things concerning their brethren who were in the land of Zarahemla.
Limhi's record from the time his people left the land of Zarahemla was brought before Ammon.
Limhi sent forty-three men into the wilderness to find the land of Zarahemla and appeal for deliverance.
The searchers failed to find the land of Zarahemla and returned after discovering a ruined land.
ambiguity_flags: Ruined land discovery not normalized here

references.text_excerpt: The king of the people in the land of Zarahemla was the one commanded to interpret with God's gift.
Limhi rejoiced when he learned the newcomers were his brethren from the land of Zarahemla.
Mosiah read the records of Zeniff from the time they left the land of Zarahemla until they returned again.
Mosiah also read the account of Alma and his brethren from the time they left the land of Zarahemla until they returned again.
references.review_notes: Zarahemla departure/return frame for Alma's record.

references.text_excerpt: Mosiah granted Alma authority to establish churches throughout all the land of Zarahemla.
There were seven churches in the land of Zarahemla, all part of one church of God.
Alma the Younger and the sons of Mosiah traveled throughout all the land of Zarahemla repairing the injuries they had done to the church.
The reign of the judges commenced throughout all the land of Zarahemla.
King Benjamin drove the Lamanites out of the land of Zarahemla after serious war and bloodshed.
Amalickiah sought to take possession of the land Bountiful and also the land northward, but was repulsed by Teancum.
The Lamanites abandoned their design of marching into the land northward and retreated into the city of Mulek.
references.review_notes: Direct northward objective abandoned after Amalickiah's death.

references.text_excerpt: Hagoth built a ship on the borders of Bountiful by Desolation and launched it into the west sea by the narrow neck leading into the land northward.
references.review_notes: Key Bountiful-Desolation-west sea gateway verse into the northward land.

references.text_excerpt: More people entered Hagoth's ships and set out again to the land northward.
Many people went forth into the land northward.
Corianton went forth in a ship to the land northward to carry provisions to the people there.
references.review_notes: Direct provisioning movement to the northward land.

references.text_excerpt: A great city was built by the narrow neck; the land southward was preserved for wilderness, and the whole face of the land northward was covered with inhabitants.
references.review_notes: Strong macro northward-southward contrast in Jaredite context.

references.text_excerpt: Nephi had been among the people in the land northward, preaching and prophesying to them.
references.review_notes: Direct missionary presence in the northward land.

references.text_excerpt: The Nephites were given the land northward to the narrow passage leading into the land southward, and the Lamanites received all the land southward.
references.review_notes: Late territorial division verse anchoring northward versus southward.

references.text_excerpt: The first parents of Coriantumr's people came from the tower, and their bones lay scattered in the land northward.
references.review_notes: Early northward reference tied to Jaredite remains.

references.text_excerpt: Alma spoke to the church established in the city of Zarahemla.
references.review_notes: Direct city-level church setting in Zarahemla.

references.text_excerpt: After speaking to the church established in the city of Zarahemla, Alma ordained priests and elders there.
They began to establish the order of the church in the city of Zarahemla.
Coriantumr was in possession of the city of Zarahemla, the strongest hold in all the land.
The Lamanites had taken the capital city, which was the city of Zarahemla, and marched through the most capital parts of the land.
Samuel pronounced woe upon this great city of Zarahemla, saying the more part of the city would harden their hearts.
The enemy durst not march down against the city of Zarahemla nor cross the head of Sidon over to Nephihah.
references.review_notes: City-target phrasing retained alongside Sidon-head crossing reference.

references.text_excerpt: Moroni addressed Pahoran in the city of Zarahemla, chief judge and governor over the land.
After fortifying exposed parts of the land, Moroni returned to the city of Zarahemla.
Moronihah took possession of the city of Zarahemla again.
Nephi's garden, tower, highway, and chief market were in the city of Zarahemla.
references.review_notes: Important internal urban-structure verse for Zarahemla.

references.text_excerpt: The people mourned that their brethren had been burned in that great city Zarahemla.
The voice declared that great city Zarahemla had been burned with fire.
It was with the land of Ammonihah as with other Nephite lands named after their first possessor.
Amulek preached unto the people who were in the land of Ammonihah.
references.review_notes: Direct preaching context in the land of Ammonihah.

references.text_excerpt: The chief judge over the land of Ammonihah and many teachers and lawyers entered the prison.
The people in the land of Ammonihah remained hard-hearted and unrepentant.
Amulek had forsaken his possessions which were in the land of Ammonihah for the word of God.
The people did not go in to possess the land of Ammonihah for many years, and it was called Desolation of Nehors.
Alma came to the city of Ammonihah and began to preach the word of God.
The people of the city of Ammonihah would not hearken unto Alma.
Alma sorrowed because of the wickedness of the people in the city of Ammonihah.
An angel commanded Alma to return to the city of Ammonihah and preach again unto the people there.
Alma returned speedily to the land of Ammonihah and entered the city by another way, on the south of the city of Ammonihah.
references.review_notes: Direct city-entry verse with south-side approach detail.

references.text_excerpt: The city of Ammonihah had been rebuilt and fortified with earthworks and a protected entrance.
Amalickiah might have caused the Lamanites to attack the Nephites at the city of Ammonihah.
The Lamanite chief captains durst not attack the Nephites at the city of Ammonihah.
The city of Noah had become strong, even to exceed the strength of the city Ammonihah.
Moroni supposed the enemy would be frightened at the city Ammonihah and therefore march to Noah instead.
references.review_notes: Ammonihah fear-target verse; keeps east-of-Sidon Lehi callback as supporting context.

references.text_excerpt: The Lamanites retreated into the wilderness and marched towards the land of Noah.
references.review_notes: Direct approach toward the land of Noah.

references.text_excerpt: The armies marched to the land of Noah, and the city of Noah had become strong, even exceeding the strength of the city of Ammonihah.
references.review_notes: Direct Noah land/city military verse with comparison to Ammonihah.

references.text_excerpt: Because the city of Noah had been the weakest part of the land, Moroni supposed the enemy would march there, and Lehi commanded the men of that city.
references.review_notes: Direct Noah city weakness/targeting verse with Ammonihah comparison.

references.text_excerpt: The land of Jershon was given for an inheritance; it was on the east by the sea and joined the land Bountiful.
The people of Ammon went down into the land of Jershon and took possession of it.
After the people of Ammon were established in the land of Jershon, there began to be continual peace throughout the land.
Korihor went over to the land of Jershon to preach among the people of Ammon.
Amulek and others withdrew and came over into the land of Jershon.
The rest of the brethren also came over into the land of Jershon.
Those cast out of the land came over into the land of Jershon, and Alma and his brethren ministered unto them.
Ammon went to the land of Ishmael, named after the sons of Ishmael, and was taken by the Lamanites there.
Ammon was carried before the king who was over the land of Ishmael.
Ammon and Lamoni returned from Middoni to the land of Ishmael, the land of their inheritance.
Lamoni caused synagogues to be built in the land of Ishmael.
Lamoni reigned over the people in the land of Ishmael and in all the land round about.
Aaron said that Ammon had gone to the land of Ishmael to teach the people of Lamoni.
The Lamanites were driven back to the city of Manti.
Helaman's next objective was to obtain the city of Manti.
The Lamanite chief captains took no thought concerning the city of Manti.
The Nephites arrived before the Lamanites at the city of Manti.
The Nephites took possession of the city of Manti without shedding blood.
Those who fled out of Moroni came to the city of Nephihah, and the people of the city of Lehi prepared to receive battle.
references.review_notes: Direct Nephihah and Lehi mobilization verse.

references.text_excerpt: Amalickiah would not suffer the Lamanites to go against the city of Nephihah to battle.
Amalickiah took possession of many cities, including the city of Nephihah.
references.review_notes: Direct east-border city list including Nephihah.

references.text_excerpt: Moroni supposed men should be sent to the city of Nephihah to assist in maintaining it.
When Moroni saw that the city of Nephihah was lost he was exceedingly sorrowful.
Moroni and Pahoran obtained possession of the city of Nephihah without the loss of one soul.
The people of Nephihah, gathered from the cities of Moroni, Lehi, and Morianton, were attacked by the Lamanites.
references.review_notes: Direct Lehi participation in the eastern-theater mobilization.

references.text_excerpt: The Lamanites journeyed by the head of Sidon that they might come into the land of Manti and take possession of it.
references.review_notes: Direct approach into Manti via the head of Sidon.

references.text_excerpt: Alma revealed that the Lamanites were marching to come over into the land of Manti.
Moroni left part of his army in Jershon and marched the remainder into the land of Manti.
The Lamanites fled again before the Nephites towards the land of Manti.
Helaman's men marched by another way towards the land of Manti.
Those compelled to flee from the land of Manti and the land round about had joined the Lamanites.
The people of the Lamanites who were in the city of Nephi were among those converted.
Amalickiah entered the city Nephi with his armies and took possession of the city.
Those not overtaken fled into the city of Nephi and called on Zeniff for protection.
The priests of king Noah durst not return to the city of Nephi.
Limhi and his people returned to the city of Nephi and dwelt in the land again in peace.
Those who escaped returned again to the city of Nephi in bondage.
references.review_notes: Direct return to city Nephi under oppression.

references.text_excerpt: Nephi invoked the Red Sea crossing as a comparison to Moses leading Israel through on dry ground.
references.review_notes: Direct Red Sea reference in an allusive context.

references.text_excerpt: The family traveled in the fertile parts of the wilderness near the borders of the Red Sea.
Nephi recalled Moses dividing the waters of the Red Sea so Israel could pass through on dry ground.
Nephi recalled the Egyptians being drowned in the Red Sea.
Isaiah's wording refers to the way of the Red Sea beyond Jordan in Galilee of the nations.
references.review_notes: Retained for completeness as a Red Sea reference, though not part of Lehi's route.

references.text_excerpt: Alma praised God for swallowing up the Egyptians in the Red Sea and leading their fathers to the promised land.
Teancum could not come because the Lamanites were upon the borders of the land by the west sea.
The Lamanites on the west sea south had gained possession of a number of Nephite cities.
Helaman marched to support the people in the borders of the land on the south by the west sea.
The people spread across the whole face of the land from the sea west to the sea east, on both the northward and southward.
Moroni threatened to pursue the Lamanites into their own land, the land of their first inheritance.
references.review_notes: Direct first-inheritance reference in wartime rhetoric.

references.text_excerpt: Moroni declared that his people would seek again the land of their first inheritance.
The record recalls wrongs suffered in the land of their first inheritance after crossing the sea.
Limhi compared the Lord's former deliverance through the Red Sea with the hoped-for deliverance of his own people.
Nephi invoked Moses and the crossing of the Red Sea as a precedent for God's power and judgment.
Moroni went forth from the land of Nephihah to the land of Lehi after regaining prisoners and taking Nephihah.
Alma recalled his father's church beginnings in the land called Mormon and baptisms in the waters of Mormon.
Those who came to the place of Mormon were baptized in the waters of Mormon.
The record celebrated Mormon, the waters of Mormon, and the forest near the waters of Mormon as the place of conversion and covenant.
Alma baptized the people after the manner he had baptized his brethren in the waters of Mormon.
The Lord blessed Alma and those who were baptized in the waters of Mormon.
Alma again preached to the people who were in the city of Ammonihah.
The Lamanites came into the borders of the land, into the city of Ammonihah, and destroyed the city.
Before the Nephites could respond, the people in the city of Ammonihah were destroyed and some around the borders of Noah were taken captive.
Helaman recalled the words Amulek spoke to Zeezrom in the city of Ammonihah.
Ammoron offered to deliver up the city of Antiparah in exchange for prisoners.
Helaman judged that the city of Antiparah could be taken by force without trading prisoners.
After Ammoron refused the exchange, preparations were made to go against the city of Antiparah.
The people of Antiparah fled to other cities, and the city of Antiparah fell into Nephite hands.
Zoram was about to flee and return to the city of Jerusalem.
Nephi beheld the great city of Jerusalem and also other cities before seeing Nazareth.
The flocks fled before the poisonous serpents towards the land southward, which the Nephites called Zarahemla.
Some of the people escaped and fled into the land southward.
Lib's people went into the land southward to hunt food after the serpents were destroyed.
Mormon gathered his people at Desolation by the narrow pass which led into the land southward.
Nephi had been in the land of Bountiful for many days when the Lord commanded him to go into the mountain.
Moroni arrived with his army at the land of Bountiful late in the twenty-seventh year.
The Nephites were driven, with Moronihah's armies, even into the land of Bountiful.
The land of Nephi ran in a straight course from the east sea to the west.
The Nephites founded the city Moroni by the east sea on the south by the line of Lamanite possessions.
The Lamanites were to harass the Nephites on the borders by the east sea and take possession of their lands.
Nephi's brethren complained that he thought he could build a ship and cross the great waters.
The shining stones gave light so the Jaredites might not cross the great waters in darkness.
Mosiah's people reported that the Mulekites had been brought across the great waters into the land where Mosiah found them.
The Lord commanded Lehi's sons to go to the house of Laban and seek the records.
Nephi went forth towards the house of Laban by night after his brothers hid outside the walls.
Nephi came near unto the house of Laban and found a man fallen drunken before him.
Corihor came up unto the land of Moron where the king dwelt and took him captive.
The land of Moron, where the king dwelt, was near the land later called Desolation by the Nephites.
Coriantumr came up unto the land of Moron and gave battle unto Lib.
Teancum received orders to attack the city of Mulek and retake it if possible.
Jacob determined to cut his way through to the city of Mulek, but Moroni and his men were stronger.
Moroni went to the city of Mulek with Lehi, took command there, and gave the city to Lehi.
The Lamanite armies were seen approaching towards the land of Ammonihah.
Helaman marched with the two thousand young men to the city of Judea to assist Antipus.
The Lamanites had taken possession of the land of Manti, or the city of Manti, along with Zeezrom, Cumeni, and Antiparah.
The Lamanites were compelled not to come against the city of Judea after Antipus received reinforcements.
Messengers cried that the Lamanites were marching towards the city of Cumeni and would destroy the people there.
The sons of the people of Ammon were with Helaman in the city of Manti and had been preserved.
The Nephites were driven back to the land of Desolation, and the Lamanites took possession of the city Desolation.
When Coriantumr came to the plains of Agosh he gave battle unto Lib and smote him until he died.
Converted Lamanites were in the land of Shilom, the land of Shemlon, the city of Lemuel, and the city of Shimnilom.
From Noah's tower one could overlook the land of Shilom and the land of Shemlon.
The king looked toward the land of Shemlon and saw the Lamanite army within the borders of the land.
Limhi proposed that they depart into the wilderness and travel around the land of Shilom.
Lehi spoke to his sons in the valley of Lemuel with power and confounded them.
Nephi said his father saw, heard, and spoke many things while dwelling in a tent in the valley of Lemuel.
The things Nephi had spoken of were done as his father dwelt in a tent in the valley of Lemuel.
Korihor came over into the land of Gideon after being carried out of another land and preached there.
Korihor went on in the same manner as he had in the land of Gideon.
The converted people of the Lamanites were in the land of Ishmael.
Ammon and his brethren came from Midian to the land of Ishmael to hold a council with Lamoni and Anti-Nephi-Lehi.
The company departed into the wilderness and traveled across the river Laman.
Alma's brethren gathered themselves together in the city of Helam when the Lamanites appeared.
The Lamanites took possession of the land of Helam after Alma's people delivered themselves up.
The Nephite armies were prepared to meet the Lamanites in the land of Jershon.
The people of Morianton were restored to the land of Morianton, and union took place with the people of Lehi.
Moroni sent Laman and a few men to the guards who were over the Nephites in the city of Gid.
references.review_notes: Direct founding verse for pre-destruction city Moroni.

references.text_excerpt: Lehi prophesied that the great city Jerusalem would be destroyed and later possessed again after captivity.
references.review_notes: Direct Old World Jerusalem destruction-and-return prophecy.

references.text_excerpt: The Lamanites, Amalekites, and Amulonites had built a great city called Jerusalem, and Aaron came there to preach.
references.review_notes: Direct New World Jerusalem city reference.

references.text_excerpt: The city of Jerusalem was among the wicked cities destroyed in the destruction cataclysms.
references.review_notes: Destruction-period reference to the New World city Jerusalem.

references.text_excerpt: The Lamanites came into the land of Moroni in the borders by the seashore and took the city of Moroni.
references.review_notes: Direct city Moroni capture verse with coastal-border framing.

references.text_excerpt: Moroni and Pahoran marched with a large body of men towards the land of Nephihah to overthrow the Lamanites in that city.
Coriantumr came to the waters of Ripliancum, and both armies pitched their tents near them before battle.

references.text_excerpt: And it came to pass that when we had gone up to the land of Jerusalem, I and my brethren did consult one with another.
references.review_notes: Derived Old World regional overlay reference.

references.text_excerpt: And after this manner of language did my father, Lehi, comfort my mother, Sariah, concerning us, while we journeyed in the wilderness up to the land of Jerusalem, to obtain the record of the Jews.

references.text_excerpt: And it came to pass in the which rebellion, they were desirous to return unto the land of Jerusalem.

references.text_excerpt: And he also spake unto them concerning the land of promise, which they had obtained—how merciful the Lord had been in warning us that we should flee out of the land of Jerusalem.

references.text_excerpt: And now, Zoram, I speak unto you: Behold, thou art the servant of Laban; nevertheless, thou hast been brought out of the land of Jerusalem, and I know that thou art a true friend unto my son, Nephi, forever.
references.review_notes: Derived Old World regional overlay reference.

references.text_excerpt: And now this I speak because of the spirit which is in me. And notwithstanding they have been carried away they shall return again, and possess the land of Jerusalem; wherefore, they shall be restored again to the land of their inheritance.
references.review_notes: Derived Old World regional overlay reference.

references.text_excerpt: I am Mormon, and a pure descendant of Lehi. I have reason to bless my God and my Savior Jesus Christ, that he brought our fathers out of the land of Jerusalem, (and no one knew it save it were himself and those whom he brought out of tha...

references.text_excerpt: And verily, verily, I say unto you that I have other sheep, which are not of this land, neither of the land of Jerusalem, neither in any parts of that land round about whither I have been to minister.

references.review_notes: Derived Old World regional overlay reference.

references.text_excerpt: And the king said: Is God that Great Spirit that brought our fathers out of the land of Jerusalem?
references.review_notes: Derived Old World regional overlay reference.

references.text_excerpt: Yea, why will he not show himself in this land as well as in the land of Jerusalem?

references.text_excerpt: Wherefore, thus saith the Lord, I have led this people forth out of the land of Jerusalem, by the power of mine arm, that I might raise up unto me a righteous branch from the fruit of the loins of Joseph.
references.review_notes: Derived Old World regional overlay reference.

references.text_excerpt: For behold, I, the Lord, have seen the sorrow, and heard the mourning of the daughters of my people in the land of Jerusalem, yea, and in all the lands of my people, because of the wickedness and abominations of their husbands.
references.review_notes: Derived Old World regional overlay reference.

references.text_excerpt: And I will not suffer, saith the Lord of Hosts, that the cries of the fair daughters of this people, which I have led out of the land of Jerusalem, shall come up unto me against the men of my people, saith the Lord of Hosts.

references.text_excerpt: Yea, behold, I write unto all the ends of the earth; yea, unto you, twelve tribes of Israel, who shall be judged according to your works by the twelve whom Jesus chose to be his disciples in the land of Jerusalem.
references.review_notes: Derived Old World regional overlay reference.

references.text_excerpt: And the remainder, having fled into the east wilderness, and having usurped the power and authority over the Lamanites, caused that many of the Lamanites should perish by fire because of their belief—

Now this martyrdom caused that many of their brethren should be stirred up to anger; and there began to be contention in the wilderness; and the Lamanites began to hunt the seed of Amulon and his brethren and began to slay them; and they...
And it came to pass that the Lamanites came with their thousands; and they came into the land of Antionum, which is the land of the Zoramites; and a man by the name of Zerahemnah was their leader.

And Lamah had fallen with his ten thousand; and Gilgal had fallen with his ten thousand; and Limhah had fallen with his ten thousand; and Jeneum had fallen with his ten thousand; and Cumenihah, and Moronihah, and Antionum, and Shiblom, a...

references.text_excerpt: And after he had been in the service of the king three days, as he was with the Lamanitish servants going forth with their flocks to the place of water, which was called the water of Sebus, and all the Lamanites drive their flocks hither, that they may have waterâ
Therefore, they did as Ammon commanded them, and he went forth and stood to contend with those who stood by the waters of Sebus; and they were in number not a few.

Now it was the practice of these Lamanites to stand by the waters of Sebus to scatter the flocks of the people, that thereby they might drive away many that were scattered unto their own land, it being a practice of plunder among them.
But others rebuked them, saying: The king hath brought this evil upon his house, because he slew his servants who had had their flocks scattered at the waters of Sebus.
Now, these are they who were converted unto the Lord:

And also of the people of the Lamanites who were in the land of Shilom, and who were in the land of Shemlon, and in the city of Lemuel, and in the city of Shimnilom. And these are the names of the cities of the Lamanites which were converted unto the Lord; and these are they that laid down the weapons of their rebellion, yea, all their weapons of war; and they were all Lamanites.

Now, as Alma was teaching and speaking unto the people upon the hill Onidah, there came a great multitude unto him, who were those of whom we have been speaking, of whom were poor in heart, because of their poverty as to the things of the world.

And now he had got the command of those parts of the Lamanites who were in favor of the king; and he sought to gain favor of those who were not obedient; therefore he went forward to the place which was called Onidah, for thither had all the Lamanites fled; for they discovered the army coming, and, supposing that they were coming to destroy them, therefore they fled to Onidah, to the place of arms

And this is not all, my son. Thou didst do that which was grievous unto me; for thou didst forsake the ministry, and did go over into the land of Siron among the borders of the Lamanites, after the harlot Isabel.
Therefore, he divided his army and brought a part over into the valley, and concealed them on the east, and on the south of the hill Riplah;
And it came to pass that they had gathered themselves together upon the top of the mount which was called Antipas, in preparation to battle.

And it came to pass that when it was night he sent a secret embassy into the mount Antipas, desiring that the leader of those who were upon the mount, whose name was Lehonti, that he should come down to the foot of the mount, for he desired to speak with him.

And now these are the cities of which the Lamanites have obtained possession by the shedding of the blood of so many of our valiant men; The land of Manti, or the city of Manti, and the city of Zeezrom, and the city of Cumeni, and the city of Antiparah.
And behold, the city of Gilgal have I caused to be sunk, and the inhabitants thereof to be buried up in the depths of the earth;

And it came to pass that Coriantumr was exceedingly angry with Shared, and he went against him with his armies to battle; and they did meet in great anger, and they did meet in the valley of Gilgal; and the battle became exceedingly sore. And it came to pass that Shared fought against him for the space of three days. And it came to pass that Coriantumr beat him, and did pursue him until he came to

And behold, the city of Laman, and the city of Josh, and the city of Gad, and the city of Kishkumen, have I caused to be burned with fire, and the inhabitants thereof, because of their wickedness in casting out the prophets, and stoning those whom I did send to declare unto them concerning their wickedness and their abominations.

Therefore, when ye are about twenty and four years old I would that ye should remember the things that ye have observed concerning this people; and when ye are of that age go to the land Antum, unto a hill which shall be called Shim; and there have I deposited unto the Lord all the sacred engravings concerning this people. And behold, ye shall take the plates of Nephi unto yourself, and the remain
And now I, Mormon, seeing that the Lamanites were about to overthrow the land, therefore I did go to the hill Shim, and did take up all the records which Ammaron had hid up unto the Lord.

And it came to pass that we did come to the city of Angola, and we did take possession of the city, and make preparations to defend ourselves against the Lamanites. And it came to pass that we did fortify the city with our might; but notwithstanding all our fortifications the Lamanites did come upon us and did drive us out of the city.
And we marched forth and came to the land of Joshua, which was in the borders west by the seashore.

And it came to pass that in the three hundred and forty and fifth year the Nephites did begin to flee before the Lamanites; and they were pursued until they came even to the land of Jashon, before it was possible to stop them in their retreat. And now, the city of Jashon was near the land where Ammaron had deposited the records unto the Lord, that they might not be destroyed. And behold I had gone

And it came to pass that in this year the people of Nephi again were hunted and driven. And it came to pass that we were driven forth until we had come northward to the land which was called Shem. And it came to pass that we did fortify the city of Shem, and we did gather in our people as much as it were possible, that perhaps we might save them from destruction.

And the remainder did flee and join the inhabitants of the city Teancum. Now the city Teancum lay in the borders by the seashore; and it was also near the city Desolation. And it was because the armies of the Nephites went up unto the Lamanites that they began to be smitten; for were it not for that, the Lamanites could have had no power over them. But, behold, the judgments of God will overtake t

And they fled again from before them, and they came to the city Boaz; and there they did stand against the Lamanites with exceeding boldness, insomuch that the Lamanites did not beat them until they had come again the second time. And when they had come the second time, the Nephites were driven and slaughtered with an exceedingly great slaughter; their women and their children were again sacrifice
And it came to pass that the Lamanites did come against us as we had fled to the city of Jordan; but behold, they were driven back that they did not take the city at that time.

And now I proceed with my record; for behold, it came to pass that the Lord did bring Jared and his brethren forth even to that great sea which divideth the lands. And as they came to the sea they pitched their tents; and they called the name of the place Moriancumer; and they dwelt in tents, and dwelt in tents upon the seashore for the space of four years.
For the brother of Jared said unto the mountain Zerin, Removeâand it was removed. And if he had not had faith it would not have moved; wherefore thou workest after men have faith.
And it came to pass that Shared fought against him for the space of three days. And it came to pass that Coriantumr beat him, and did pursue him until he came to the plains of Heshlon.

And it came to pass that the armies of Coriantumr did press upon the armies of Shiz that they beat them, that they caused them to flee before them; and they did flee southward, and did pitch their tents in a place which was called Ogath.

And now I write somewhat concerning the sufferings of this people. For according to the knowledge which I have received from Amoron, behold, the Lamanites have many prisoners, which they took from the tower of Sherrizah; and there were men, women, and children. And the husbands and fathers of those women and children they have slain; and they feed the women upon the flesh of their husbands, and th

And again, my son, there are many widows and their daughters who remain in Sherrizah; and that part of the provisions which the Lamanites did not carry away, behold, the army of Zenephi has carried away, and left them to wander whithersoever they can for food; and many old women do faint by the way and die. And the army which is with me is weak; and the armies of the Lamanites are betwixt Sherriza

And after they had done this thing, they did murder them in a most cruel manner, torturing their bodies even unto death; and after they have done this, they devour their flesh like unto wild beasts, because of the hardness of their hearts; and they do it for a token of bravery.

And Alma and his people departed into the wilderness; and when they had traveled all day they pitched their tents in a valley, and they called the valley Alma, because he led their way in the wilderness. Yea, and in the valley of Alma they poured out their thanks to God because he had been merciful unto them, and eased their burdens, and had delivered them out of bondage; for they were in bondage,

And the voice of the Lord came to Ammon, saying: Thou shalt not go up to the land of Nephi, for behold, the king will seek thy life; but thou shalt go to the land of Middoni; for behold, thy brother Aaron, and also Muloki and Ammah are in prison. Now it came to pass that when Ammon had heard this, he said unto Lamoni: Behold, my brother and brethren are in prison at Middoni, and I go that I may de

And it came to pass that they saw that the people would harden their hearts, therefore they departed and came over into the land of Middoni. And they did preach the word unto many, and few believed on the words which they taught. Nevertheless, Aaron and a certain number of his brethren were taken and cast into prison, and the remainder of them fled out of the land of Middoni unto the regions round
And also of the people of the Lamanites who were in the land of Middoni;

And now, after the space of two years, and after the death of Shared, behold, there arose the brother of Shared and he gave battle unto Coriantumr, in which Coriantumr did beat him and did pursue him to the wilderness of Akish. And it came to pass that the brother of Shared did give battle unto him in the wilderness of Akish; and the battle became exceedingly sore, and many thousands fell by the s
And it came to pass that Lib did smite the army of Coriantumr, that they fled again to the wilderness of Akish.

And they pitched their tents in the valley of Corihor; and Coriantumr pitched his tents in the valley of Shurr. Now the valley of Shurr was near the hill Comnor; wherefore, Coriantumr did gather his armies together upon the hill Comnor, and did sound a trumpet unto the armies of Shiz to invite them forth to battle.
